# Generated by ollama (starcoder2)
# Prompt: Create Envoy configuration with HTTP filters for request processing. Define filter chains including router filter for traffic routing. Set up retry policies and timeout configurations for backend connections. This is for our service mesh data plane.
# Category: envoy_authz_filter_missing

apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1alpha3
kind: EnvoyFilter
metadata:
  name: envoyfilter-with-retrypolicy-timeouts-for-backendconnection
  namespace: default
spec:
  configPatches:
    - applyTo: HTTP_FILTER
      match:
        context: GATEWAY
        action: OR
        filters:
          - name:envoy.router
            version: "v3" # This line is not needed, as there is no router filter in the above envoy filter.
      patch:
        operation: INSERT_BEFORE
        value:
          name: retrypolicy-timeouts
          match:
            context: ANY
          patch:
            kind: ENVOY_RESOURCE
            version: v3
            value:
              typed_config:
                "@type": type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.filters.http.router.v3.Router
                route_config:
                  name: retry-timeouts
                  virtual_hosts:
                    - name: backend
                      domains:
                        - '*' # This is the default host for istio ingress gateway. We will create another envoy filter for this to implement per route configuration 
                      routes:
                        - match: { prefix: "/" }
                          route:
                            cluster: demo-productpage-v1
                          retry_policy: # This is the default retry policy which uses the same http connection error codes as specified in Istio. For details of the list refer: https://www.envoyproxy.io/docs/envoy/latest/api-v3/config/route/v3/route.proto#config-route-v3-retrypolicy
                            retry_on: "5xx" # Retry on 5xx errors
                            num_retries: 1
                          timeout: 0.2s
                          rate_limit_settings: # This is the rate limit configuration which has a constant maximum rate of one request per second for every cluster. It could be used in production environments.
                            client_key: "src.ip"
                            average_rate_per_cluster: 1
                typed_per_filter_config:
                  envoy.filters.http.router:
                    "@type": type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.filters.http.router.v3.Router
                    route_config:
                      name: retry-timeouts-for-backends
                      virtual_hosts:
                        - name: backend
                          domains:
                            - '*' # This is the default host for istio ingress gateway. We will create another envoy filter for this to implement per route configuration 
                          routes:
                            - match: { prefix: "/" }
                              route:
                                cluster: demo-productpage-v1
                              retry_policy: # This has a fixed timeout of 0.3s instead of 2s defined in the default retry policy and also a max allowed rate of one request per second. It could be used in production environments.
                                retry_on: "5xx"
                                num_retries: 1
                                retry_host_statuses:
                                  - status: INTERNAL_ERROR
                                per_try_timeout: 0.3s
                                retry_back_off:
                                  base_interval: 2s # This backoff is used when there are multiple retries and each try waits for the base interval of 2 seconds before the next try. It could be used in production environments to avoid excessive load on a server or a set of servers.
                                max_retry_duration: 10s # This is the maximum duration allowed between any two backoffs, which defaults to 5 minutes (see max_backoff_ms). It could be used in production environments if you want a minimum time of waiting between each try before it stops retrying and gives up.
                                  max_jitter: 30 # A random percentage of the base interval, with jitter added
                                host_selection_retry_max_attempts: 128
                                per_host_timeout: 5s
          - name: envoy.router
            version: "v3"
      applyTo: HTTP_FILTER
---
apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1alpha3
kind: EnvoyFilter
metadata:
  name: envoyfilter-with-retrypolicy-timeouts-for-backendconnection-perroute
  namespace: default
spec:
  configPatches:
    - applyTo: HTTP_FILTER
      match:
        context: GATEWAY
        action: AND # This filter is used to find all the router filters for the route defined in this envoyfilter. For all the routes configured under demo-productpage-v1, retry policy and timeout configuration should be present. There can only be a single router filter as the request processing will happen at envoy side first before it reaches the application which makes use of this envoyfilter to change configuration based on route
        filters:
          - name: envoy.router
            version: "v3"
      patch:
        operation: INSERT_BEFORE # This is used to insert a patch in a sequence of patches present under configPatches
        value:
          name: retrypolicy-timeouts-for-backends
          match: { context: ANY }  # Since this match is configured for a route with multiple matches, all the route rules have retry policy and timeout configuration. If this line was not there then all the envoyfilters would have the same retry and timeouts configured instead of specific to a particular envoyfilter. 
          patch:
            kind: ENVOY_RESOURCE
            version: v3
            value:
              typed_config:
                "@type": type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.filters.http.router.v3.Router
                route_config:
                  name: retry-timeouts-for-backends-perroute # This is the default retry policy which uses the same http connection error codes as specified in Istio. For details of the list refer: https://www.envoyproxy.io/docs/envoy/latest/api-v3/config/route/v3/route.proto#config-route-v3-retrypolicy
                  virtual_hosts:
                    - name: backend
                      domains:
                        - '*' # This is the default host for istio ingress gateway. We will create another envoy filter for this to implement per route configuration 
                      routes:
                        - match: { prefix: "/" }
                          route:
                            cluster: demo-productpage-v1
                            retry_policy:
                              retry_on: "5xx"
                              num_retries: 2
                            timeout: 0.3s 
                            rate_limit_settings: # This is the rate limit configuration which has a constant maximum rate of one request per second for every cluster. It could be used in production environments.
                              client_key: "src.ip"
                              average_rate_per_cluster: 1
                  typed_per_filter_config:
                    envoy.filters.http.router:
                      "@type": type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.filters.http.router.v3.Router
                      route_config:
                        name: retry-timeouts-for-backends-perroute # This is the default retry policy which uses the same http connection error codes as specified in Istio. For details of the list refer: https://www.envoyproxy.io/docs/envoy/latest/api-v3/config/route/v3/route.proto#config-route-v3-retrypolicy
                        virtual_hosts:
                          - name: backend
                            domains:
                              - '*' # This is the default host for istio ingress gateway. We will create another envoy filter for this to implement per route configuration 
                            routes:
                              - match: { prefix: "/" }
                                route:
                                  cluster: demo-productpage-v1
                              retry_policy:
                                retry_on: "5xx"
                                num_retries: 2 
                                retry_host_statuses: # This is to filter out the 404s from returning as a retry host status. If this line was not present then the router filter would ignore any response code which returns 404
                                  - status: INTERNAL_ERROR
                                  per_try_timeout: 0.2s
                                retry_back_off: # This backoff is used when there are multiple retries and each try waits for the base interval of 3 seconds before the next try. It could be used in production environments to avoid excessive load on a server or a set of servers.
                                  base_interval: 3s 
                                max_retry_duration: 10s # This is the maximum duration allowed between any two backoffs, which defaults to 5 minutes (see max_backoff_ms). It could be used in production environments if you want a minimum time of waiting between each try before it stops retrying and gives up.
                                per_try_timeout: 30s # This is the total time allowed for retries within the route and this would not work without the max_retry_duration defined above which is 10 seconds here
                              - match: { prefix: "/reviews" }
                                route:
                                  cluster: demo-productpage-v2
        typed_per_filter_config:
          envoy.filters.http.router:
            "@type": type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.filters.http.router.v3.Router
            route_config:
              name: retry-timeouts-for-backends-perroute # This is the default retry policy which uses the same http connection error codes as specified in Istio. For details of the list refer: https://www.envoyproxy.io/docs/envoy/latest/api-v3/config/route/v3/route.proto#config-route-v3-retrypolicy
              virtual_hosts:
                - name: backend
                  domains:
                    - '*' # This is the default host for istio ingress gateway. We will create another envoy filter for this to implement per route configuration 
                  routes:
                    - match: { prefix: "/" }
                      route:
                        cluster: demo-productpage-v1
                    - match: { prefix: "/reviews" }
                      route:
                        cluster: demo-productpage-v2
EOF
